rulings.Case Title: THEODORE KRYSINSKI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Substitute Successor For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 19, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to substitute successor for deceased plaintiff is continued on the court¿s own motion to July 26, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 to give defendant Kerr Corporation an opportunity, if it wishes, to provide any supplemental opposition to the motion in light of the submission of the authorization signed by Arlene Krysinski with the reply papers. The authorization, which was signed prior to the filing of the motion, should have been included with the moving papers. Kerr Corporation may file supplemental opposition papers no later than July 22. No further papers may be filed on this motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILEY UTTERBACK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 19, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Aerojet-General Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence that Wiley Utterback was exposed to any asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ discovery responses did not give defendant any basis to believe that plaintiffs proceeded on any liability theory other than a premises theory. Accordingly, defendant cannot be faulted for confining its motion for summary judgment to a premises theory and not explicitly addressing a ¿direct negligence¿ theory. Defendant¿s objection to the entirety of the declaration of Russell Utterback is sustained. The court finds that plaintiffs¿ response to special interrogatory 10 dated April 7, 2011 was willfully false and thus subject to exclusion per Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corporation (2004) 124 Cal. App. 4th 1315. Since Wiley Utterback and Russell Utterback are brothers, they worked alongside each other throughout their careers and both are represented by the same law firm in pending asbestos exposure lawsuits (and plaintiff¿s law firm also represents Russell Utterback¿s former wife in another pending lawsuit based on asserted take home exposure derivative from Russell Utterback¿s own exposure), assuming the truth of Russell Utterback¿s declaration filed with the opposition papers, the omission of any reference in response to special interrogatory 10 to Russell Utterback is not reconcilable with the statement in the same response that a ¿reasonable and good faith effort¿ was made to obtain responsive information and no information other than what was stated was learned. = (220/HEK)
